summary: Finished decompiling sysdolphin baselib mobj by replacing the remaining asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.s with matched C in src/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.c, removing the asm object from obj_files.mk, and promoting several previously anonymous MObj-related symbols into named APIs used by other asm files. The PR also adjusted baselib headers for TExp constructor prototypes, TObj id signedness, and the HSD_MObj layout.

key_files.role: Main matched C implementation; gained MObjMakeTExp, HSD_MObjCompileTev, setup/unset, toon/shadow texture management, allocation/removal, release/amnesia, and class init logic.
Deleted 1069-line assembly file after mobj.c became the source of the object.
Removed extra HSD_MObj fields to match the recovered object layout.
Added prototypes for HSD_TExpTev and HSD_TExpCnst needed by mobj.c.
Changed HSD_TObj id from u32 to s32.
Relabeled calls from func_80363C38 to HSD_MObjGetTObj.
Relabeled calls from func_80363B8C to HSD_MObjSetToonTextureImage.
Relabeled small-data globals lbl_804D7624/lbl_804D7620 as tobj_shadows/tobj_toon.
Relabeled small-data globals lbl_804D7624/lbl_804D7620 as tobj_shadows/tobj_toon.
Relabeled small-data globals lbl_804D7624/lbl_804D7620 as tobj_shadows/tobj_toon.

what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.s and removed its object from obj_files.mk, indicating the mobj module was fully matched in C.
Expanded src/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.c by roughly 384 added lines, adding the remaining MObj logic that had been in assembly.
Added MObjMakeTExp, which builds HSD_TExp TEV expressions from an HSD_MObj and linked HSD_TObj list, handling diffuse/alpha/specular paths and texture flag classes 0x50, 0x20, and 0x80.

Added HSD_MObjCompileTev, which frees stale tevdesc/texp state, temporarily appends global shadow textures or prepends the toon texture depending on rendermode bits, assigns TObj resources, compiles the TExp, and restores the temporary list link.

Added HSD_MObjSetup and MObjSetupTev, which initialize TEV/material state, set material color and shininess, temporarily inject tobj_shadows/tobj_toon into setup, call HSD_TObj setup helpers, and set render mode with custom PE.
Added global HSD_TObj* tobj_shadows and HSD_TObj* tobj_toon, plus static default_class and current_mobj storage in mobj.c.
Added HSD_MObjSetToonTextureImage, HSD_MObjSetDiffuseColor, HSD_MObjSetAlpha, HSD_MObjGetTObj, HSD_MObjAddShadowTexture, and HSD_MObjDeleteShadowTexture.
Added MObjRelease, MObjAmnesia, and MObjInfoInit class-method wiring for release, amnesia, load, make_texp, setup, unset, and setup_tev.
Changed MObjUpdateFunc alpha math from using extern lbl_804DE430 to literal 1.0F - val->fv.
Changed HSD_MObjLoadDesc to use assert_line(353, mobj) instead of explicit __assert labels.
Removed tobj_toon, tobj_gradation, tobj_backlight, and z_offset fields from HSD_MObj in mobj.h.
Added HSD_TExpTev and HSD_TExpCnst declarations in texp.h.
Changed HSD_TObj::id from u32 to s32 in tobj.h.
Updated numerous still-assembly callers to use recovered symbols such as HSD_MObjGetTObj, HSD_MObjSetToonTextureImage, HSD_MObjSetDiffuseColor, HSD_MObjSetAlpha, tobj_shadows, and tobj_toon.
smart_moves: The PR did not only add C; it also removed the asm object from the build, which is the clearest evidence that the module was fully matched and no duplicate object remained.
Recovered global names were propagated into unrelated asm files, improving future decomp readability without requiring those files to be decompiled first.

The implementation preserves temporary linked-list mutation carefully: shadow textures are appended through a saved pointer-to-pointer and then detached, while toon texture temporarily points at the original TObj list.
MObjAmnesia clears default_class and global tobj_toon/tobj_shadows when the corresponding class info is forgotten, avoiding stale class/global state.
The use of static storage for default_class/current_mobj and exported globals only where cross-file references exist separates internal class state from symbols that other modules actually touch.

The code keeps force_active unused strings such as "hsdIsDescendantOf(info, &hsdMObj)" and "mobj->rendermode&RENDER_SPECULAR", suggesting deliberate preservation of string/data artifacts needed for matching.
decomp_lessons: When a baselib source file becomes fully matched, update obj_files.mk in the same PR to remove the corresponding asm object; otherwise the match status and build linkage remain ambiguous.
Finishing one core module can unlock many reliable symbol names in other assembly files; relabel callers immediately to create better anchors for later work.
For HSD class modules, matching often requires the full lifecycle, not only public helpers: load, alloc, setup, unset, release, amnesia, and info-init vtable wiring all interact.
Temporary modifications to linked resource lists should be restored before returning; mobj uses pointer-to-pointer bookkeeping to append tobj_shadows and then set the appended link back to NULL.
Small header layout corrections can be part of a match. Here, deleting four HSD_MObj fields and changing TObj id signedness were necessary structural/header cleanups tied to matching.
Prefer known project assertion helpers like assert_line when they reproduce the original callsite better than manually declaring string-label externs.
Preserving otherwise-unused literals with force_active pragmas can be necessary when matching baselib modules that contain assert/debug strings.
Expose prototypes in headers as soon as source uses previously undeclared baselib helpers; this PR added HSD_TExpTev and HSD_TExpCnst to texp.h rather than relying on implicit or local declarations.
naming_conventions: Public MObj APIs use the HSD_MObj prefix, e.g. HSD_MObjCompileTev, HSD_MObjSetToonTextureImage, HSD_MObjSetDiffuseColor, HSD_MObjSetAlpha, HSD_MObjGetTObj, HSD_MObjRemove, HSD_MObjAlloc, HSD_MObjAddShadowTexture, and HSD_MObjDeleteShadowTexture.
Internal class or method functions use MObj-prefixed names without HSD_, e.g. MObjMakeTExp, MObjSetupTev, MObjLoad, MObjRelease, MObjAmnesia, and MObjInfoInit.
Global texture-object state is named by purpose as tobj_shadows and tobj_toon instead of lbl_804D7624/lbl_804D7620.
Class info follows the existing hsdMObj naming style, with default_class kept as static module state.
Recovered callsites replace func_80363C38 with HSD_MObjGetTObj, func_80363B8C with HSD_MObjSetToonTextureImage, func_80363C10 with HSD_MObjSetDiffuseColor, and func_80363C2C with HSD_MObjSetAlpha.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the mobj.s assembly file after porting its 1069 lines into C, rather than keeping partial inline assembly.
Relabeled branch-and-link targets in asm callsites while preserving instruction bytes and addresses, confirming the recovered symbol names without changing caller behavior.
Relabeled small-data loads from lbl_804D7624@sda21 and lbl_804D7620@sda21 to tobj_shadows@sda21 and tobj_toon@sda21 across ft_unknown_006.s, grmaterial.s, and code_80266F3C.s.
Used exact floating constants such as 1.0F and initialized descriptor literals for tobj_toon_desc to reproduce generated data.
Used #pragma force_active blocks to keep otherwise-unused strings in the object.
Used pointer-to-pointer list traversal patterns in HSD_MObjCompileTev, HSD_MObjSetup, and HSD_MObjDeleteShadowTexture, likely to match original control flow and generated code.
The TObj id type change to s32 likely supports signed sentinel values or codegen around id comparisons; the diff only shows the type change, so the exact matching reason is not explicit.

review_feedback.note: No inline review comments or substantive reviewer feedback were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/sysdolphin/baselib/mobj.c for renames of rendermode constants or cleanup of magic bits 0x50, 0x20, 0x80, 0x1000, and 0x04000000.
Search for subsequent header changes to HSD_MObj, HSD_TObj, and HSD_TExp to see whether this layout/prototype work was refined.
Look for PRs decompiling grmaterial, ft_unknown_006, code_80266F3C, eflib, grpura, grstadium, ftparts, or mncharsel that reused the MObj symbols introduced here.
Search for tobj_shadows and tobj_toon usage to understand the global shadow/toon texture lifecycle across baselib and gameplay modules.
Check whether later work replaced magic TEV/TObj constants in MObjMakeTExp with named enums or macros.
